title: ranunculus amplexicaulis var. plantagineus
intro: Ranunculus amplexicaulis var. plantagineus, also known as Creeping Buttercup, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant with small, yellow flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and protecting against bacterial and fungal infections. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and it is often used topically to treat minor skin issues and internally to support overall health and well-being.
